programs.title: Woman at the end of the world
programs.desc:
Martyna Wojciechowska has been wandering the world for many years and looking for interesting people who share their unique stories with her.The traveler not only talks about their everyday lives, but also raises important social issues.In the tenth season of the program he will visit, among others:Spitsbergen - the largest island of the Svalbard archipelago, where it is night for a quarter of the year.He will also reach out to the Wodaabe community living in Chad, and in El Salvador he will tackle the difficult issue of restrictive anti-abortion regulations.
Martyna Wojciechowska talks to Morena Herrera, who fights for women's rights in El Salvador.The country ranks first in terms of the number of murders of women.About 500 such cases are reported annually, with a population of less than 3 million.More than half of the victims of violence do not seek help and do not reveal the truth even to their closest family members.Moreover, El Salvador has one of the most restrictive anti-abortion laws in the world.Termination of pregnancy is always illegal.If a woman loses a child in unclear circumstances, she immediately becomes a suspect.The penalty for her ranges from 2 to 8 years, and if found guilty, she may be imprisoned for 50 years.A doctor can be sentenced to 6 to 12 years for performing an abortion.Anyone who knew about the situation but did not inform the authorities is also complicit.

programs.title: The Extraordinary States of Procopius
programs.desc: More than 170,000 members of this tribe live on the Navajo Nation reservation.The picturesque areas located in four states were supposed to be an enclave of free Indians.In fact, they often resemble vast ghettos.The history of the Navajo is rich and painful, as Marcin Prokop discovers during a visit to one of the last living cryptographers from this tribe, who fought in the Pacific during World War II.The next hero is Loren Anthony, who grew up in the mountains, living in primitive conditions.As a teenager, he formed a metal band, which allowed him to express generational frustrations.After various life challenges, Loren decided to focus on music, charity work and supporting his community.For many young residents of the reserve, this type of creativity is a way to shout out wrongs and escape from the sad reality.Navajos fight stereotypes, valuing the value of their community and traditions, which encourages them to remain in the difficult areas of the reservation.

programs.title: The Extraordinary States of Procopius
programs.desc: A three-day UFO festival takes place every year in a small town in Pennsylvania, attracting extraterrestrial civilization enthusiasts from all over America.This place is not accidental, because most reports of encounters with aliens are reported in this area.Although interest in UFOs appears to be waning in favor of technology and artificial intelligence threats, extraterrestrial enthusiasts remain active.They are organizing and writing petitions to the government asking for the disclosure of secret files.Although UFOs are most often associated with Roswell and Area 51, the center of this phenomenon is currently located in a provincial town near Pittsburgh and in Washington state.Marcin Prokop intends to meet with UFO enthusiasts and researchers to assess the evidence for the existence of cryptids and extraterrestrial civilizations.

programs.title: By truck across the United States
programs.desc: Dawid Andres will once again take advantage of the opportunity to combine delivering goods with exploring American nature, architecture and culture.With him, viewers will visit, among others:Atlanta and check out the Daytona track.
